Current Operations

    Other Operations

  • Taking Action Against Iran-Backed Militia Commanders Threatening U.S. Personnel US Dept. of State 17 Apr 2026 -- The United States is taking decisive action to hold accountable seven commanders of Iran-aligned terrorist militia groups who have planned and directed attacks against U.S. personnel, facilities, and interests in Iraq.
  • Economic Fury Targets Iran-Backed Iraqi Militia Commanders US Treasury 17 Apr 2026 -- Today, the U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) designated seven Iraqi militia commanders responsible for planning, directing, and executing attacks against U.S. personnel, facilities, and interests in Iraq.

    • Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • April 16: Iran-US truce extends to Lebanon while US seeks second round of talks Press TV 17 Apr 2026 -- On Day 48 of the US-Israeli war on Iran and the resistance axis, diplomatic momentum intensified as Washington announced a 10-day ceasefire in Lebanon, an outcome Iranian and Hezbollah officials credited to the resistance's battlefield steadfastness.

  • Iranian Navy prevented enemy carrier groups, marines from entering Sea of Oman: Commander Press TV 17 Apr 2026 -- A top Iranian naval commander says Iranian forces successfully prevented US and Israeli aircraft carrier strike groups and marine forces from entering the Sea of Oman.
  • 'New order' in Strait of Hormuz: IRGC Navy mandates authorization for all vessels Press TV 17 Apr 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) Navy says a "new order" is now in place over the strategic Strait of Hormuz, outlining strict new regulations for all maritime traffic.
  • Three Iranian oil tankers exit Strait of Hormuz, defying US 'naval blockade' Press TV 17 Apr 2026 -- Three Iranian oil tankers carrying a combined five million barrels of crude oil have become the first loaded vessels to leave the Persian Gulf through the Strait of Hormuz since the US-imposed "naval blockade" came into effect earlier this week.
  • Iran reopens Strait of Hormuz to commercial traffic following Lebanon ceasefire Press TV 17 Apr 2026 -- Following the implementation of a ceasefire in Lebanon, Iran has announced the reopening of the strategic Strait of Hormuz to commercial vessels, signaling a successful culmination of its decisive military and diplomatic campaign against US and Israeli aggression.
  • Exclusive: IRGC locked 16 cruise missiles on US warships in Strait of Hormuz before they retreated Press TV 17 Apr 2026 -- In new details of last week's operation in the Strait of Hormuz, Press TV has learned that the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) Navy locked more than a dozen cruise missiles onto aggressive US warships moments before they hastily retreated.
  • 'Hands on trigger': IRGC says ready alongside Iran's Army to counter any aggression Press TV 17 Apr 2026 -- Iran's Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) says it stands ready alongside the Army to deliver a "regret-inducing" response to the US and Israeli regime if they renew their criminal act of aggression against the country.

  • Trump Says US-Iran Deal 'Very Close' After Tehran Declares Strait Of Hormuz Open RFE/RL 17 Apr 2026 -- Iran said it has opened the Strait of Hormuz to commercial shipping traffic for the remainder of a 10-day cease-fire between Israel and Lebanon, and US President Donald Trump again said Washington and Tehran could reach a deal to end the war with Iran in the near future.
  • Trump says he considers visiting Pakistan if U.S., Iran reach peace deal People's Daily 17 Apr 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump said Thursday he would consider visiting Pakistan if a peace deal is reached between the United States and Iran to end the weekslong conflict.
  • Trump Says US 'Very Close' To A Deal With Iran RFE/RL 17 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said the United States and Iran are "very close" to a deal to end the war and that US and Iranian negotiators could meet for a second round of talks this coming weekend.

  • Iran Says Strait Of Hormuz 'Completely Open' During Israel-Lebanon Cease-Fire; Trump Says US Blockade Of Iranian Ports Remains In Place RFE/RL 17 Apr 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi says the Strait of Hormuz, a key waterway for global trade, will be "completely open" for the duration of the cease-fire signed between Lebanon and Israel.
  • Iranian Media Close To IRGC Criticize Hormuz Announcement RFE/RL 17 Apr 2026 -- Media outlets close to Iran's hard-line Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps (IRGC) have criticized what they say has been the "silence" of Iranian officials regarding an agreement with the United States about the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.
  • 'Social media don't govern Strait of Hormuz': Qalibaf refutes Trump's claims on strategic waterway Press TV 17 Apr 2026 -- Iran's Majlis (Parliament) speaker has categorically refuted successive false claims made by US President Donald Trump regarding the Strait of Hormuz and the rules set by the Islamic Republic regarding transit through the strategic waterway.
  • Strait of Hormuz traffic to follow Iran-designated route under Iranian coordination: FM spox Press TV 17 Apr 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesperson, Esmaeil Baghaei, says maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz must strictly follow routes designated by Tehran and operate under full Iranian coordination.
  • Tehran warns US of consequences for broken vows amid Hormuz Strait conditional reopening Press TV 17 Apr 2026 -- As Iran conditionally reopens the strategic Strait of Hormuz following a decisive diplomatic and military victory, Tehran has issued a warning to the United States that any failure to honor its commitments will be met with severe consequences.
  • US, Israel have no option but to surrender to Iranian nation, armed forces: Top cmdr. Press TV 17 Apr 2026 -- Commander of Iran's highest operational command unit says the country's enemies and ill-wishers, particularly the United States and the Israeli regime, have no option but to yield to the greatness and strength of the Iranian Armed Forces and resilient nation.
  • Iranian official rejects any temporary ceasefire, says new Hormuz protocol possible Press TV 17 Apr 2026 -- Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister has stressed that the Islamic Republic opposes any temporary ceasefire and insists the cycle of war must end permanently.
  • Leader's advisor outlines four 'uncompromisable' pillars of Iran's future Press TV 17 Apr 2026 -- A top advisor to the Leader of the Islamic Revolution, Ayatollah Seyyed Mojtaba Khamenei, has defined Iran's internal stability and its regional alliances among the "invaluable fruits" of national resilience in the face of the enemies' threats.
  • Iran fully prepared to combat any threat, aggression with 'iron will': Army chief Press TV 17 Apr 2026 -- Commander-in-Chief of the Iranian Army Major General Amir Hatami says the country is fully prepared with "iron will" to confront any threats and aggression against the Islamic Republic.

  • Statement attributable to the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General - on the Strait of Hormuz UN Secretary-General 17 Apr 2026

  • Why Hopes That China Can Push Iran Toward Peace May Be Misplaced RFE/RL 17 Apr 2026 -- When China's special envoy to the Middle East recently gave a colorful account of a dangerous overland journey through a war zone, it was a departure from the bland language and boilerplate peace plans often used by the country's diplomats.
  • Bringing US, Iran back to talks is top priority: China's top diplomat Global Times 17 Apr 2026 -- Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi said on Thursday that the top priority is to return the United States and Iran to the negotiating table and seek a political solution.
  • Chinese envoy says veto of draft UN Security Council resolution on Strait of Hormuz helps prevent further escalation and creates conditions for talks Global Times 17 Apr 2026 -- President,

  • The President: We are ready to contribute to ensuring security in the Strait of Hormuz President of the Republic of Lithuania 17 Apr 2026 -- President Gitanas Nausėda took part in a virtual meeting of heads of state convened by French President Emmanuel Macron and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer to discuss security in the Strait of Hormuz.
  • Press statement by the Chancellor at the conference on securing the Strait of Hormuz - For stability in the Middle East Germany - Federal Government 17 Apr 2026 -- Press statement by the Chancellor at the conference on securing the Strait of Hormuz
  • France Says European Countries Could Ensure Safe Passage Through Strait Of Hormuz RFE/RL 17 Apr 2026 -- French Defense Minister Catherine Vautrin said on April 17 that European countries such as Belgium, the Netherlands, and France possess mine-clearance capabilities that could help secure safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz.
  • Joint Statement by President Macron and Prime Minister Starmer, Co-chairs of the International Summit on the Strait of Hormuz. Élysée - President of France 17 Apr 2026 -- Today, France and the United Kingdom convened 51 countries for an international summit on the Strait of Hormuz. The meeting underlined the determination of the international community to support freedom of navigation, to stand up for international law, and to protect global economic stability and energy security.
  • International Law Is Still Worth Defending Government of Norway 17 Apr 2026 -- As the United States and Israel rain bombs down on Iran, with the stated intent of subduing one of the world's most brutal regimes, international law is collateral damage. The US-Israeli strikes clearly defy fundamental rules governing the use of force, as do Iranian missile and drone attacks against civilians in Israel and elsewhere in the region. But these are just the latest in a long train of such violations. And given the rise of "might makes right" foreign policies, they might not be the last.
  • Written message from Prime Minister TAKAICHI for virtual Leaders Meeting of the Freedom of Navigation in the Strait of Hormuz hosted by France and the United Kingdom Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 17 Apr 2026
  • Partner Countries Need to Work Together to Remove the Dangers in the Strait of Hormuz and Help Protect Freedom of Navigation Everywhere in the World - The President President of Ukraine 17 Apr 2026
  • Address by the President to Participants of the Virtual Meeting on Freedom of Navigation in the Strait of Hormuz President of Ukraine 17 Apr 2026

  • Ryan Kirkley: From Oil To Bitcoin, Markets Shift With Strait Of Hormuz News RFE/RL 17 Apr 2026 -- Cryptocurrency markets surged and oil traders recalibrated after signals that the Strait of Hormuz is reopening, even as military tensions and policy contradictions persist.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Nordhaus Underscores National Guard's Indispensable Role DoD News 17 Apr 2026 -- In the span of a single year, the men and women of the National Guard conducted precision airstrikes to dismantle Iran's nuclear program, rescued dozens of children from rising Texas floodwaters, repelled cyber intrusions targeting critical infrastructure and sent thousands of troops to restore order and safety in American cities.
  • Guard Unit 'Turns Blue,' Symbolizing Mobile Infantry Transition DoD News 17 Apr 2026 -- The Nevada Army National Guard's 1st Battalion, 221st Cavalry Regiment transitioned from a mounted cavalry formation to a mobile infantry battalion during a "turning blue" ceremony at the Clark County Armory in Las Vegas, April 12.
  • Air Force, Army, Marines Unite for Joint Air Traffic Control Training DoD News 17 Apr 2026 -- Airmen, soldiers and Marines completed two weeks of airfield training at Robins Air Force Base, Georgia, learning valuable skills while highlighting the power of military interoperability.
  • US delays delivery of weapons to European allies as war on Iran drains stockpile: Report Press TV 17 Apr 2026 -- The United States has reportedly informed its European allies of weapons delivery delays as the aggression that it waged jointly with the Israeli regime against Iran depletes the country's stockpile.
  • Air Force advances secure AI capabilities with IL5 approval for VIA's eJARVIS US Air Force 17 Apr 2026 -- The Department of the Air Force has awarded Via Science, Inc., a leading provider of AI technologies, Impact Level 5 Authority to Operate for its eJARVIS application.

  • US Lawmaker Wilson Flags Western Chips Still Flowing Into Russian, Iranian Drones RFE/RL 17 Apr 2026 -- A senior Republican lawmaker has raised concerns over continued US-origin components appearing in Russian weapon systems amid Ukrainian reports that recent drone strikes show evidence of Western microelectronics manufactured as recently as 2025.

  • NATO Allies and Industry representatives meeting on Energy and Critical Undersea Infrastructure security NATO 17 Apr 2026 -- On Friday (17 April 2026), NATO Allies and industry representatives met at NATO Headquarters in Brussels, to discuss the implications of developments in the Middle East for energy and Critical Undersea Infrastructure (CUI) security.

  • A sinking fleet: russia's largest losses in the Black Sea and the Sea of Azov during the war Ukraine MOD 17 Apr 2026 -- Ukraine has achieved what was once considered impossible even in military theory: a country without a major navy has effectively defeated the aggressor in waters it once regarded as its home domain.

  • China's defense ministry urges New Zealand to restrain frontline forces, stop disruptive acts after NZ aircraft's harassment in Yellow Sea and East China Sea Global Times 17 Apr 2026 -- When asked to comment on the repeated recent activities of a New Zealand air force military aircraft near China's peripheral airspace, which have disrupted numerous civil aviation flights, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said at a regular press conference on Friday that a P-8A anti-submarine patrol aircraft of the New Zealand air force conducted continuous close-in reconnaissance and harassment in the airspace and waters of the Yellow Sea and East China Sea. Such actions undermine China's security interests, heighten the risks of misunderstanding and miscalculation, and severely disrupt civil aviation operations in relevant airspace.
  • China takes firm and forceful response to NZ air force patrol aircraft's reconnaissance and harassment in Yellow Sea and East China Sea: FM Global Times 17 Apr 2026 -- When asked to comment on the repeated recent activities of a New Zealand air force military aircraft near China's peripheral airspace, which have disrupted numerous civil aviation flights, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said at a regular press conference on Friday that a P-8A anti-submarine patrol aircraft of the New Zealand air force conducted continuous close-in reconnaissance and harassment in the airspace and waters of the Yellow Sea and East China Sea. Such actions undermine China's security interests, heighten the risks of misunderstanding and miscalculation, and severely disrupt civil aviation operations in relevant airspace.

  • President highlights defense budget urgency during military inspection CNA 17 Apr 2026 -- President Lai Ching-te (賴清德) on Friday called for the swift passage of a special defense budget during an inspection of the Army Infantry Training Command in Kaohsiung, stressing the need to strengthen Taiwan's military preparedness.
  • Cheap drones, costly defenses: Middle East war offers warning for Taiwan CNA 17 Apr 2026 -- The ongoing war in the Middle East is exposing a costly imbalance in modern warfare, as the United States and its allies burn through multimillion-dollar air defense missiles to intercept waves of low-cost drones -- a dynamic that analysts warn could pose an even greater threat to Taiwan in a conflict with China.

  • Preparing Australia for future strategic challenges Australia DOD 17 Apr 2026 -- An Australian Defence Force focused on deterrence, self-reliance and stronger partnerships underpins the 2026 National Defence Strategy (NDS), announced on April 16.
  • ADF supports Solomon Islands after Cyclone Maila Australia DOD 17 Apr 2026 -- The Australian Defence Force has rapidly mobilised to support Solomon Islands communities following the devastating impact of severe Tropical Cyclone Maila.
  • Buffalo is back Australia DOD 17 Apr 2026 -- More than 80 years after its formation, the Buffalo call sign has returned home to 12 Squadron at RAAF Base Edinburgh.

  • Canadian Armed Forces to conduct inaugural active participation in Exercise BALIKATAN Canada 17 Apr 2026 -- Marking a significant step in Canada's Indo-Pacific engagement, the Canadian Armed Forces (CAF) will actively participate in Exercise BALIKATAN, an annual military exercise in the Philippines, from April 20 to May 8, 2026.

  • Germany - Integrated Combat System, Supporting Equipment, and Support US Dept. of State 17 Apr 2026 -- The U.S. Department of State has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of Germany to Integrated Combat System, Supporting Equipment, and Support. The estimated total cost is $11.9 billion.

  • French LSS 3 Émile Bertin Launched at Saint Nazaire OCCAR 17 Apr 2026 -- The third French Logistic Support Ship (LSS), Émile Bertin, was officially launched today at the Chantiers de l'Atlantique shipyard, marking a major milestone in the Franco-Italian Logistic Support Ship (LSS) Programme managed by OCCAR.

  • CULMINATION OF FIRST EDITION OF NAVAL COMMANDERS' CONFERENCE 2026 India PIB 17 Apr 2026 -- The first edition of the biannual Naval Commanders' conference 2026 was conducted from 14-17 Apr 2026 at Nausena Bhawan, New Delhi.
  • ARMY COMMANDERS' CONFERENCE CONCLUDES IN NEW DELHI India PIB 17 Apr 2026 -- The biannual Army Commanders' Conference (ACC) which commenced on 13 April 26, culminated on 16 April 26.

  • Visit to the Ministry of Defense and visit to Mitsubishi Electric Corporation Kamakura Works by the NAC Permanent Representatives Ministry of Defense of Japan 17 Apr 2026
  • The 4th Japan-U.S. Defense Industrial Cooperation, Acquisition, and Sustainment (DICAS 2.0) Ministry of Defense of Japan 17 Apr 2026
  • China urges Japan to make clean break with militarism: Defense Spokesperson PRC MND 17 Apr 2026 -- "We urge the Japanese side to stop its retrogressive moves and make a clean break with militarism. Otherwise, it will lose the trust of its Asian neighbors and the international community," said Senior Colonel Zhang Xiaogang, spokesperson for China's Ministry of National Defense (MND), at a press briefing on Friday.

  • "Anatolian Phoenix - 2026" exercise wraps up in Türkiye Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 17 Apr 2026 -- "Anatolian Phoenix - 2026" International Search and Rescue Exercise held in Konya, Türkiye, has successfully completed, Azerbaijan's Ministry of Defense told AZERTAC.

  • Defence Minister's meeting with the President of Elbit Systems Company Romania - Ministry of National Defence 17 Apr 2026 -- On Friday, April 17th, Minister of National Defence, Radu Miruță, had a meeting, at the MoND headquarters, with Bezhalel Machlis, President of Elbit Systems Company, in the presence of H.E. Lior Ben Dor, the Ambassador of the State of Israel to Romania.

  • The largest military exercise of 2026, Spring Storm, is approaching Estonia Defence Forces 17 Apr 2026 -- On May 4, a nearly month-long military exercise Spring Storm 2026 will begin, involving over 12,000 troops from Estonia and allied nations at its peak.
  • Minister Pevkur in Vilnius: Estonia and Lithuania's defence spending sets an example for allies Estonia MFA 17 Apr 2026 -- Estonian Minister of Defence Hanno Pevkur met his Lithuanian counterpart, Robertas Kaunas, in Vilnius to discuss defence cooperation, preparations for the NATO summit in Ankara, and Lithuania's defence priorities during its forthcoming Presidency of the Council of the European Union.

  • Minister of National Defence met with Minister of Defence of Moldova Lithuania MND 17 Apr 2026 -- Minister of National Defence Robertas Kaunas met with Minister of Defence of Moldova Anatolie Nosatîi in Vilnius.

  • Friendly nations can join 'Balikatan' drills - AFP Philippine News Agency 17 Apr 2026 -- The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) on Thursday underscored its willingness to accept other friendly nations to take part in the annual "Balikatan" exercises provided that they comply with the necessary "legal and operational foundations" for the entry of foreign military troops.
  • PH joins US-led Pax Silica; plan for industrial hub in Luzon unveiled Philippine News Agency 17 Apr 2026 -- The Philippines has joined the United States-led Pax Silica initiative, a strategic coalition among 14 states to create a secure supply chain for semiconductors and artificial intelligence (AI) technology.

  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Belizean Prime Minister Briceño US Dept. of State 17 Apr 2026
  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Mauritian Foreign Minister Ramful US Dept. of State 17 Apr 2026

Defense Industry

  • Contracts for April 17, 2026 DoD
  • U.S. Department of State Concludes $36 Million Settlement Resolving Export Violations by General Electric Company US Dept. of State 17 Apr 2026 -- The U.S. Department of State has concluded an administrative settlement with General Electric Company (GE Aerospace) to resolve 116 violations of the Arms Export Control Act (AECA), 22 U.S.C. § 2751 et seq., and the International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR), 22 C.F.R. parts 120-130.

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 17 April 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 17 Apr 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots | Roaring Lion (Lebanon)

  • 10-day ceasefire between Israel, Lebanon goes into effect People's Daily 17 Apr 2026 -- A 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon took effect at midnight between Thursday and Friday local time (2100 GMT), following an earlier announcement by U.S. President Donald Trump.
  • Israel-Lebanon Cease-Fire Officially Takes Effect, Trump Urges Hezbollah To Act 'Nicely' RFE/RL 17 Apr 2026 -- A 10-day cease-fire agreed by Lebanon and Israel went into effect at midnight local time, with journalists reporting "heavy gunfire" and rocket launches that may have been celebratory firings as the truce began.

  • PM Netanyahu: "At the request of my friend, President Trump, we have agreed to a temporary ceasefire in Lebanon" Israel - Prime Minister 17 Apr 2026 -- "There were two threats emanating from Lebanon, a near threat and a distant threat: (...) We have removed both of these threats."

  • Hezbollah says truce owed to Iran, warns of 'treacherous enemy' Press TV 17 Apr 2026 -- Hezbollah says its fighters still have their "hand on the trigger" and are monitoring any enemy sabotage after the announcement of a 10-day ceasefire, as the Lebanese group reviewed 45 days of resistance against Israeli forces.

  • Statement by UNICEF on the killing of two water truck drivers in the Gaza Strip UNICEF 17 Apr 2026
  • UNESCO grants 'enhanced protection' to cultural heritage sites in the Middle East UN News 17 Apr 2026 -- Since the Middle East war started on 28 February, several sites of major cultural significance have come under attack in Israel, Iran and Lebanon. Ensuring their protection is the task of the UN agency for education, science and culture, UNESCO.
  • Key restrictions continue to hamper aid response in Gaza: UN People's Daily 17 Apr 2026 -- While aid cargo is entering Gaza, restrictions on "dual-use" items, including equipment to dispose of unexploded ordnance, continue to constrain the relief response, UN humanitarians said Thursday.

  • 'Enough is enough': Trump says US prohibiting Israel from launching further attacks on Lebanon Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 17 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said Friday that Washington is actively prohibiting Israel from carrying out additional attacks on Lebanon as a nascent ceasefire takes hold, according to Anadolu Agency.

  • Iran: Lebanon ceasefire major diplomatic victory for Axis of Resistance Press TV 17 Apr 2026 -- Iran has warmly welcomed the announcement of a ceasefire in Lebanon, underlining that the halt to aggression against the country forms an integral part of the broader ceasefire understanding reached between the Islamic Republic and the United States, with constructive mediation by Pakistan.

  • European leaders urge full implementation of Israel-Lebanon ceasefire People's Daily 17 Apr 2026 -- European leaders on Thursday called for the full implementation of the 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon and urged efforts toward a lasting peace.
  • Statement by Foreign Minister Eide on ceasefire in Lebanon Government of Norway 17 Apr 2026 -- 'I fully support the ceasefire in Lebanon as announced by President Trump. This is an important and much needed step towards de-escalation of the current crisis in the region. I commend the USA for facilitating the conversations leading to this announcement, as well as the governments of Lebanon and Israel for seeking a diplomatic solution to the conflict', said Foreign Minister Espen Barth Eide.
  • Ceasefire Agreement on the Situation in Israel and Lebanon (Statement by Foreign Minister MOTEGI Toshimitsu) Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 17 Apr 2026
  • OIC Affirms Support and Solidarity with Palestinian Prisoners in Israeli Occupation Prisons Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 17 Apr 2026 -- On the occasion of Palestinian Prisoners' Day, which falls on 17 April every year, the General Secretariat of the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) expressed deep concern over the deteriorating conditions of Palestinian prisoners in Israeli occupation prisons, numbering more than 9500 prisoners, including 73 female prisoners and 350 children, in addition to detainees from the Gaza Strip, whose number is unknown
  • Int'l community welcomes Israel-Lebanon ceasefire, urges continued efforts toward peace People's Daily 17 Apr 2026 -- A 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon took effect on Friday, following an announcement earlier by U.S. President Donald Trump.
  • Foreign Secretary statement on the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire UK FCDO 17 Apr 2026 -- Foreign Secretary statement welcoming the announcement of a ceasefire in Lebanon
  • Comment by Foreign Ministry Spokeswoman Maria Zakharova on the developments in Lebanon Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 17 Apr 2026
  • MOFA Spokesperson's Statement on Ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon ROK MFA 17 Apr 2026
  • PM call with President Aoun of Lebanon: 17 April 2026 Gov.UK 17 Apr 2026 -- The Prime Minister called the President of Lebanon, Joseph Aoun, from Paris this afternoon.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: South Sudan, Lebanon, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Afghanistan, Haiti UNOCHA 17 Apr 2026

  • UNHCR: 2025 was deadliest year yet for maritime movements of Rohingya refugees UNHCR 17 Apr 2026 -- In 2025, nearly 900 Rohingya refugees were reported missing or dead in the Andaman Sea and Bay of Bengal, making it the deadliest year on record for maritime movements in South and South‑East Asia, according to data from UNHCR, the UN Refugee Agency.
  • Record number of Rohingya refugees died at sea in 2025: UNHCR UN News 17 Apr 2026 -- In 2025, nearly 900 Rohingya refugees were reported missing or dead in the Andaman Sea and Bay of Bengal, making it the deadliest year on record in South and Southeast Asia, the UN refugee agency, UNHCR, said on Friday.
  • Note to Correspondents: in response to questions on Myanmar UN Secretary-General 17 Apr 2026

  • Sudan: Three quarters of women feel unsafe as war rages on UN News 17 Apr 2026 -- Across war-torn Sudan, women and girls "are telling a consistent story of continued experience of danger, and risks for gender-based violence" whether when fleeing to safety or arriving at displacement camps, a senior official with the UN reproductive and sexual health agency UNFPA said on Friday.
  • Sudan: Quintet Statement on the Sudan Conference Civilian Call for an End to the War and Advancement of Peace European External Action Service (EEAS) 17 Apr 2026 -- The Quintet (the African Union, the Intergovernmental Authority on Development, the League of Arab States, the European Union, and the United Nations) welcomes the achievement made on the 15 April, marking the third anniversary of the conflict in Sudan, of a joint civilian call for de-escalation and an end to the war. In Berlin, within the framework of the International Sudan Conference, a diverse group of Sudanese civilian actors came together, many for the first time in three years, to sit at the same table and articulate a shared vision for peace.
  • Third International Sudan Conference France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 17 Apr 2026
  • Foreign Ministers' meeting on the third anniversary of the war in Sudan: Norway provides NOK 470 million in humanitarian and long-term aid Government of Norway 17 Apr 2026 -- The conflict in Sudan has developed into the world's largest humanitarian crisis, with gross abuses against civilians and serious geopolitical consequences, both within and outside the region.
  • Marking Three Years of Conflict in Sudan with Sanctions and Appeal for Humanitarian Truce US Dept. of State 17 Apr 2026 -- April 15th marked three years since the devastating civil war began in Sudan between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF).
  • Treasury Sanctions Recruitment Network Enabling War in Sudan, Calls for Humanitarian Truce US Treasury 17 Apr 2026 -- Today, following the third year of the devastating civil war in Sudan between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the paramilitary group, the Rapid Support Forces (RSF), the U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) imposed sanctions on five individuals and entities involved in recruiting and deploying former Colombian military personnel to Sudan to fight on behalf of the RSF.

  • 'Slaughtered like goats': Despair and abandonment in South Sudan UN News 17 Apr 2026 -- Senior UN officials painted a sorry picture of South Sudan on Friday at the Security Council, describing political turmoil, rising violence, hunger and disease, amid budget cuts that are limiting the ability of the UN peacekeeping mission to protect civilians.
  • United Nations Mission in South Sudan Vital Yet Hampered under Strain of Budget Cuts, Security Council Speakers Stress, amid Support for Strong, Clear Mandate UN Security Council 17 Apr 2026 -- The United Nations presence in South Sudan is both critical and constrained, the Security Council heard today, as speakers offered their visions for how the Organization's peacekeeping mission should operate amid steep financial and operational challenges in the world's youngest, yet highly fragile, nation.
  • South Sudan: Reports of burial sites highlight hidden cost of renewed violence, UN Commission calls for proper handling and investigation OHCHR 17 Apr 2026 -- The UN Commission on Human Rights in South Sudan today expressed grave concern over reports that bodies have been discovered in Jonglei State during recent road works, warning that the true human cost of the country's renewed violence may be significantly greater than currently known.
  • The only solution to the current crisis in South Sudan is through the immediate cessation of hostilities: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 17 Apr 2026 -- Statement by Ambassador Archie Young, UK Deputy Permanent Representative to the UN, at the UN Security Council meeting on South Sudan.
  • UN Relief Chief warns Security Council, South Sudan "stands at a dangerous crossroads" UNOCHA 17 Apr 2026 -- Briefing to the Security Council on South Sudan by Tom Fletcher, Under-Secretary-General for Humanitarian Affairs and Emergency Relief Coordinator

  • Azerbaijan's Defense Ministry: New military infrastructure facilities established in liberated territories Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 17 Apr 2026 -- Pursuant to the instruction of President of the Republic of Azerbaijan, Supreme Commander-in-Chief of the Armed Forces Ilham Aliyev ongoing measures to strengthen the Azerbaijan Army's material and technical capabilities, as well as to construct and modernize military infrastructure, are being successfully advanced, the Ministry of Defense told AZERTAC.

  • The Republic of Ecuador reaffirmed on Friday its support for the autonomy initiative presented by Morocco in 2007, describing it as the only serious, credible, and realistic resolution to the regional dispute over the Moroccan Sahara. Morocco Ministry of Foreign Affairs 17 Apr 2026

  • WFP urges support to protect gains in fight against entrenched food insecurity in Haiti WFP 17 Apr 2026 -- With new data showing more than half of all Haitians continue to face acute food insecurity, the United Nations World Food Programme (WFP) is urgently calling for support to protect recent fragile gains in the fight against hunger and to foster stability in the struggling Caribbean nation.
  • More than half of Haitians continue to face food crisis UN News 17 Apr 2026 -- 5.8 million Haitians, or roughly 52 per cent of the population, are facing crisis levels of food insecurity, or worse. Of those, more than 1.8 million are dealing with emergency levels, which means they are exhausting their last assets and unable to meet even basic food needs.

News Reports

  • Chicago Man Sentenced to 25 Years in Prison for Conspiring to Provide Material Support to Foreign Terrorist Organization US Dept. of Justice 17 Apr 2026 -- A Chicago man was sentenced yesterday to 25 years in federal prison for conspiring to provide material support to the Islamic State of Iraq and al-Sham (ISIS) by using social media to encourage attacks on ISIS's enemies and recruit ISIS members.

  • What Is HAYI, The Shadowy Islamist Group Claiming Attacks Across Europe? RFE/RL 17 Apr 2026 -- A little-known Islamist group has claimed a string of attacks on Jewish sites and Western financial institutions across Europe since early March.

  • Meeting with permanent members of the Security Council President of Russia 17 Apr 2026 -- The President held a briefing session with permanent members of the Security Council via videoconference.
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's remarks during the 9th Ministerial Meeting of Russia and Central Asia, Moscow, April 17, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 17 Apr 2026
  • Press release on CIS Foreign Ministers Council Meeting Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 17 Apr 2026
  • Remarks by Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov at a news conference held jointly with CIS Secretary-General Sergey Lebedev following the CIS Council of Foreign Ministers, Moscow, April 17, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 17 Apr 2026
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's remarks during an expanded meeting of the CIS Foreign Ministers Council, Moscow, April 17, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 17 Apr 2026
  • Comment by Foreign Ministry Spokeswoman Maria Zakharova on the 80th anniversary of the International Court of Justice Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 17 Apr 2026
  • Foreign Ministry Spokeswoman Maria Zakharova's reply to a media question regarding statements by the French Embassy in Moscow Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 17 Apr 2026
  • Shaheen, Schumer, Warren Statement on Trump Administration Extending Sanctions Relief for Russian Oil US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 17 Apr 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, Chuck Schumer (D-NY), Senate Democratic Leader, and Elizabeth Warren (D-MA), Ranking Member of the Senate Banking, Housing and Urban Affairs Committee, issued the below statement condemning the Trump Administration for extending the ill-conceived Russia General License 134 which removed sanctions risk for any person purchasing Russian oil loaded on vessels as of March 12, 2026. This renewal comes only two days after U.S. Secretary of Treasury Scott Bessent claimed at a White House press conference that, "We will not be renewing the general license on Russian oil."
  • Russian Lawyer Who Lashed Out At Putin Released From Mental Hospital RFE/RL 17 Apr 2026 -- Ilya Remeslo, a longtime pro-Kremlin lawyer who was admitted to a psychiatric hospital after abruptly directing strongly worded criticism at Russian President Vladimir Putin, says he has been released after a month in the facility in St. Petersburg.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on April 17, 2026 PRC MofA 17 Apr 2026
  • Xi congratulates Guelleh on re-election as president of Djibouti PRC MND 17 Apr 2026 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping on Friday sent a congratulatory message to Ismail Omar Guelleh on his re-election as president of Djibouti.
  • Joint Press Statement Between Foreign Ministers of the People's Republic of China and the Republic of Namibia PRC MofA 17 Apr 2026 -- At the Invitation of H.E. Wang Yi, Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and Minister of Foreign Affairs of the People's Republic of China, Hon. Selma Ashipala-Musavyi, Minister of International Relations and Trade of the Republic of Namibia, undertook an official visit to China from April 11 to 18, 2026.
  • China unveils major 2026 space missions, deepens global cooperation; expert says lunar and reusable rocket programs mark next growth phase Global Times 17 Apr 2026 -- China National Space Administration (CNSA) on Friday outlined plans for this year's China Space Day celebrations while unveiling a series of major missions for 2026, including crewed Shenzhou spacecraft flights and flight tests for multiple reusable rockets. A Chinese expert said the agenda shows that both state-led and commercial space programs are advancing rapidly, with lunar exploration expected to become a key focus in the next stage of development.
  • Shenzhou-21 astronauts complete third series of extravehicular activities People's Daily 17 Apr 2026 -- The Shenzhou-21 crew aboard China's orbiting space station completed their mission's third series of extravehicular activities (EVAs) on Friday, according to the China Manned Space Agency (CMSA).

  • PLA Eastern Theater Command tracks, monitors Japanese vessel transiting Taiwan Straits, ensures effective control: spokesperson Global Times 17 Apr 2026 -- Senior Colonel Xu Chenghua, spokesperson of the PLA Eastern Theater Command said on Friday that from 4:02 to 17:50 on April 17, the Japanese destroyer JS Ikazuchi transited the Taiwan Straits, sending wrong signals to "Taiwan independence" separatist forces. The PLA Eastern Theater Command (ETC) deployed naval and air assets to track and monitor the vessel throughout the process, ensuring an effective control of the situation, according to a statement released by the PLA Eastern Theater Command on its WeChat account.
  • Japan compounds wrongdoing, Chinese FM responds after Japanese Self-Defense Force vessel enters Taiwan Straits on Friday Global Times 17 Apr 2026 -- The Chinese military has handled the activities of the Japanese Self-Defense Force vessel in the Taiwan Straits in accordance with the law and relevant regulations, the Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Friday in response to a question about a Japanese Self-Defense Force vessel entered the Taiwan Straits on Friday.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 17 Apr 2026

  • PM call with Prime Minister Magyar of Hungary: 17 April 2026 Gov.UK 17 Apr 2026 -- The Prime Minister spoke to new Prime Minister of Hungary, Péter Magyar, from Paris this afternoon.
  • PM meeting with President Macron of France: 17 April 2026 Gov.UK 17 Apr 2026 -- The Prime Minister met the President of France, Emmanuel Macron, in Paris this morning, ahead of talks with partners on the Strait of Hormuz.

  • Press conference - Viva Refinery, Geelong Prime Minister of Australia 17 Apr 2026

  • Canada provides new assistance to help Cubans Canada 17 Apr 2026 -- Cuba is facing a deepening humanitarian crisis marked by repeated nationwide power grid failures, prolonged blackouts that last many hours across the island and acute fuel shortages. The ongoing economic and energy crisis has severely disrupted access to food, clean water, health care and other essential services.

  • First India-China Bilateral Consultations on SCO matters India MEA 17 Apr 2026
  • Transcript of Weekly Media Briefing by the Official Spokesperson (April 17, 2026) India MEA 17 Apr 2026

  • G7 Rapid Response Mechanism (RRM) releases report on joint monitoring of 2025 Moldovan parliamentary elections Canada 17 Apr 2026

  • Statement by the Revolutionary Government Cuba Ministry of Foreign Affairs 17 Apr 2026 -- "Cuba lives under the constant siege of the United States government, whose threats have escalated in recent months. Adding to the ruthless energy siege—which has worsened the genocidal blockade policy over the past six decades—are statements by representatives of the U.S. governmental elite threatening military aggression."

  • Norway provides additional 15 million kroner in emergency aid to Somalia Government of Norway 17 Apr 2026 -- People in Somalia are being hit hard by severe hunger. The combination of drought, conflict, rising prices and reduced international aid has led to a dramatic deterioration in the food situation in Somalia. Millions of people lack access to sufficient and nutritious food, and needs are rising rapidly.

  • Shockwaves of Middle East war reach Caribbean as food prices soar UN News 17 Apr 2026 -- Six weeks since war erupted in the Middle East, the shockwaves have spread to the Caribbean region, already pushed to the brink, amid fears of a looming El Niño-linked climate disaster.

  • World News in Brief: Myanmar amnesty, rising needs in Afghanistan, another power loss at Ukraine nuclear plant UN News 17 Apr 2026 -- Authorities in Myanmar released the country's ousted president from prison on Friday, along with some 4,000 other people, as part of an amnesty to mark the traditional New Year festival.

  • As the 'world court' turns 80, Guterres says law must prevail over force UN News 17 Apr 2026 -- The world is at a "moment of crisis" and countries must reaffirm commitment to international law amid rising violations and geopolitical tensions, UN Secretary-General António Guterres said at an event on Friday to commemorate the 80th anniversary of the International Court of Justice (ICJ).
